mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate over the prior quarter signals easier discounting and lower recession risk, which raises equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the relationship reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, markets interpret weakening labor demand as an early recession warning, causing risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce nonnegative 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average 20d return after the trigger is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumption and housing demand, which improves near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal state from RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform either series alone in 60d forward returns, or if the combined signal has negative average forward return over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A broad drop in INDPRO without a matching decline in payrolls signals output weakness before labor deterioration becomes visible, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month through earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average while PAYEMS is flat or rising
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO weakness accompanied by stable PAYEMS does not predict lower 20d SPX returns than INDPRO weakness with falling PAYEMS, or if the triggered signal has positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDPC1 growth over the prior quarter indicates resilient aggregate demand and earnings support, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next two months. This signal fails when inflation shocks dominate growth news.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60d SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL inflation is rising faster than its prior 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising above its recent trend, tighter real financing conditions compress valuation multiples and depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate/high-inflation regime does not yield negative 20d SPX returns on average, or if returns in the triggered state are not worse than in high-rate-only or inflation-only states.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS combined with a rising UNRATE identifies a labor-market inflection that precedes earnings revisions and multiple compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up state does not underperform all other labor states on 60d forward returns, or if the signal is not negative in at least 55% of matched episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in RSAFS above its recent trend signals improving nominal spending and corporate revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth rate turns positive versus prior month
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS momentum is not followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the average 5d return after the trigger is below the unconditional mean.
